steps.title: Isolate the term with b
steps.content:
Start with the given equation $a = \frac{3}{11}b + 6$. Subtract 6 from both sides to isolate the coefficient-$b$ term:

$$a - 6 = \frac{3}{11}b$$

steps.title: Multiply by the reciprocal coefficient
steps.content:
To isolate $b$, multiply both sides by $\frac{11}{3}$ (the reciprocal of $\frac{3}{11}$):

$$b = \frac{11}{3}(a - 6)$$

Distribute the factor: $b = \frac{11}{3}a - \frac{11}{3} \cdot 6 = \frac{11}{3}a - 22$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
